name: Las Vegas Coin Company
description_short: Las Vegas Coin Company buys and sells coins, bullion, collectibles, jewelry, and art. They operate a single location and emphasize fair pricing and customer education in a no-pressure environment.
description_long:
Las Vegas Coin Company is a specialty retail and buying business located at 9555 South Eastern Ave Suite 140 in Las Vegas, Nevada. The company is owned by John Jankowski, who emphasizes decades of experience in the coins and collectibles industry. They recently relocated to their current Eastern Avenue location near I-215 and maintain only one physical storefront.

The company's primary services include buying and selling coins (including rare Carson City coinage, proof sets, mint sets, and graded coins), bullion (gold, silver, platinum, and palladium), collectibles (vintage toys, sports memorabilia, military medals, autographs, and more), jewelry (gold, sterling silver, diamonds, and broken jewelry), and art (oil paintings, sculptures, bronzes, lithographs, and serigraphs). They also accept currency including $500 and $1000 bills, and offer online shopping options.

Las Vegas Coin Company differentiates itself through its stated mission of providing "extraordinary service in a no-pressure environment" and explicitly avoiding hidden fees. The owner encourages comparison shopping, inviting customers to get quotes from competitors first and then return for comparison. They position themselves as willing to make a fair profit while treating customers professionally and fairly. The company has reportedly dispersed multiple estates and collections for clients, some resulting in "sizeable sums" of money.

A notable caveat is that competitors have used their name in advertising, which the company acknowledges creates confusion online. Their business model relies on purchasing items at wholesale rates and reselling them, which means buyback offers will be lower than retail prices—a standard trade-off inherent in pawn and collectibles buying operations.
address: 9555 S Eastern Ave #140, Las Vegas, NV 89123

company_info.headquarters: 9555 S Eastern Ave #140, Las Vegas, NV 89123

pros: Single, verifiable location eliminates branch confusion; owner explicitly addresses competitors using their name
Diverse inventory spanning coins, bullion, collectibles, jewelry, and art attracts varied seller profiles
Mission statement explicitly rejects hidden fees and pressured sales tactics
Encourages price comparison with competitors before purchasing, suggesting confidence in their offers
Claims experience dispersing estates and collections for significant returns to clients
Offers online shopping option in addition to in-store transactions
Actively educates customers before transactions to ensure informed decisions
cons: Buyback prices for items will be significantly below retail value, as with all pawn/collectibles shops
Company acknowledges being confused with competitors in search results, creating potential friction for customers
Website content is cut off mid-sentence in the 'About Us' section, suggesting incomplete or outdated web presence
No published pricing guides, grading standards, or valuation methods listed on website
Limited hours (10am-4pm at time of website capture) may not accommodate all customer schedules
best_for: Estate executors and heirs looking to liquidate collections for fair market value
Bullion and precious metals investors seeking to buy or sell gold, silver, platinum, or palladium
Collectors with rare coins, sports memorabilia, vintage toys, or authenticated items to sell quickly
Individuals with jewelry, watches, or art pieces they wish to liquidate without retail transaction delays
diagnosis: Las Vegas Coin Company is best suited for people liquidating collections, estates, or bullion who want to comparison-shop local offers and value transparency over convenience. The main caveat is that all buyback prices will be wholesale rates (typically 40-70% of retail depending on item type), and customers should expect to receive less than they would selling privately or at auction—though with the benefit of immediate payment and no transaction fees.
services: Buy coins (proof sets, mint sets, graded coins, Carson City coinage, silver dollars, currency)
Buy precious metals bullion (gold, silver, platinum, palladium)
Buy collectibles (vintage toys, sports memorabilia, military medals, autographs, Coca-Cola items)
Buy jewelry (gold jewelry, sterling silver, diamonds, broken jewelry, watches)
Buy art (oil paintings, sculptures, bronzes, lithographs, serigraphs, limited editions)
Sell coins, bullion, collectibles, jewelry, and art through retail storefront
Online shopping for coins and collectibles
Customer education regarding collectible preservation and value
meta_description: Las Vegas Coin Company buys and sells coins, bullion, collectibles, jewelry, and art with fair pricing and customer education.
